structure.rationale: Landing tariff: one CTA-driven page concentrating all credibility (heritage, brand portfolio, two physical showrooms, contact) toward a single conversion goal — drive Oakland walk-in foot traffic to the Kohler Showroom at 415 40th Street.

sections.name: Hero
sections.purpose: Anchor message — 'Specialty plumbing for the East Bay since 1980' — with primary CTA to visit the Kohler Showroom; rejects the category-default emergency-call hero pattern.

sections.name: Brands We Carry
sections.purpose: Kohler, Grohe, A.O. Smith, Takagi — paired with the product category each brand covers (kitchen/bath fixtures, faucet/shower systems, tankless water heaters). Merges brief's required `brands` and `what_we_sell` into one section to respect landing's 5-section ceiling.

sections.name: Two Showrooms in Oakland
sections.purpose: Treat the Kohler Showroom and Bathroom Showroom as a physical destination — fixture vignettes from inside 415 40th Street, framed as 'come touch the premium brands' rather than a paragraph mention.

sections.name: Three Generations on 40th Street
sections.purpose: Forty-six years, same East Bay block, third-generation family ownership — heritage as visual structure (founding year, named generations) instead of buried About-page paragraph. Load-bearing trust signal vs. franchise rollups and big-box.

sections.name: Visit or Call
sections.purpose: Address, Mon–Fri 7:00–4:30 hours, phone 510-652-7437 (contractor-priority), and a low-key custom-request form. Phone-first secondary CTA respects how trade buyers actually transact.

image_briefs.prompt_seed: Editorial interior vignette inside an Oakland specialty plumbing showroom: a polished Kohler chrome and brass kitchen faucet on a honed soapstone counter, late-afternoon warm side light from an industrial-sash window, soft shadow on a cream plaster wall, a single neatly coiled copper supply line catching highlight in the foreground; restrained, magazine-quality, no people, no stock-photo smile, no plumbing-clipart props, no blue-and-white retail signage. Mood: trade-grounded heritage, third-generation specialty supplier — not a chain store.

arbiter_reasoning: Phase 1 single Synthesizer (no trio + Arbiter yet) with conscious deviation from the hardcoded conservative vector. The brief and niche intel jointly mandate an editorial-heritage direction that the default 60s-swiss-modernism / neutral-temperature preset would actively contradict: warm cream base, trade serif wordmark in the Cormorant/Domaine/Tiempos family, deep forest-green 'trade trust' accent, and explicit anti-Inter / anti-modern-SaaS-sans guidance. Accordingly: era_reference = 2024-editorial (heritage typographic restraint), color_temperature = warm, formality dialed to 0.75 (trade authority but neighborly tone-keyword), decoration held at 0.1 (anti-clipart, anti-1990s tropes), energy at 0.35 (restraint over enthusiasm). Typography mapped to open-source proxies — Cormorant Garamond for display (faithful to the requested Cormorant family, Google Fonts) and Source Serif Pro for body (sustains editorial register without violating the 'no Inter / no modern SaaS sans' rule for a non-tech-B2B niche). Color tokens preserve the BelBes #EB0000 invariant as `primary` while the brief's dark accent (#1F3A2E forest green) lives in `accent_secondary` and Kohler-black (#0A0A0A) carries body text — all consistent with 'no bright gradients, no neons.' Sitemap collapses the brief's six required_sections to five (`brands` absorbs `what_we_sell`) to respect the landing-tariff section ceiling without losing brand-portfolio surface area; merger documented above. Composition deliberately left empty per COMP-001/COMP-002 sequencing — no component IDs invented.
